title: epipactis helleborine subsp. viridans
intro: Epipactis helleborine subsp. viridans, also known as the green helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and grass-like leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Epipactis helleborine subsp. viridans include its potential to help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its ability to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
